observations.summary: The review-code step's opencode runner timed out (rc:124) during the initial run, output was salvaged (mid-stream narration), and the step was classified as 'success' despite no evidence of code review completion — the story file's Review Findings section contains only NFR findings, no code review triage results or applied patches.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 2: {"ts":"2026-07-06T05:06:50Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Code review (3.9) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes","stderrTail":""}. Journal step_end for review-code (2026-07-06T05:06:51.839Z, status: success, durationMs: 5402355) — responseExcerpt is mid-stream narration: 'The diff is 1479 lines — well under the 3000-line threshold, so no chunking needed. Per your overrides, I'll skip the pre-review checkpoint. Let me get the full diff content (including the untracked e2e test file) and read the triage/present steps.' Story file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-9-terminate-idle-sandboxes-mid-conversation.md Review Findings section (lines 332-368) contains only NFR Audit findings from review-nfrs — no code review findings, no triage results, no applied patches. No trace exists for this run (no trace directory at _bmad-output/pipeline/traces/3.9-a1-20260706T022258/). Trends show this run is the max for review-code (maxMs: 5402355 vs avgMs: 2093464).

observations.summary: Out-of-scope stale red-phase headers from earlier stories are flagged but not fixed by review-tests — 4th occurrence (3.4, 3.7, 3.8, 3.9); the 3.7 update_step guard works for in-scope markers (3 fixed in 3.9) but the agent scopes 'fix stale transitional markers directly' to the current story, deferring out-of-scope markers to a separate validation that never comes.
observations.evidence: test-review-validation-report-3-9.md:126-135 — 'Out-of-Scope Findings (NOT Story 3.9)': SlashCommandPicker.test.tsx:10 (Story 3.2, 'TDD RED PHASE: All tests are skipped (it.skip). Remove skips' but tests are active) and ToolPill.test.tsx:11 (Story 3.4, 'TDD RED PHASE — tests are skipped until implementation lands.' but tests are active). Report states 'they were not modified because they are outside the Story 3.9 scope' and recommends 'A separate validation for the relevant stories (3.2 / 3.4) should update these headers.' Journal step_end for review-tests confirms 3 in-scope headers fixed. Ledger entry 36 (Story 3.8) records the same pattern: '2 out-of-scope stale headers from Story 1.3 remain unresolved.' Playbook review-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-05T08:14:39.210Z, updatedByRun: 3.7-a1-20260704T225804) says 'Fix stale transitional markers directly' without scoping, but agent interprets as current-story-only.

observations.summary: The implement-story step did not proactively apply the .unref() timer hygiene pattern documented in project-context.md when extending IdleTimeoutService with a 15-minute mid-session timer — 3rd occurrence (2.2, 3.8, 3.9); the 3.8 update_step ('apply non-functional patterns proactively') was applied at the start of this run but did not prevent the recurrence because the instruction is too passive.
observations.evidence: Story file NFR Audit section (lines 344-350): '[NFR-Audit][HIGH] Missing .unref() on idle timeout timer — the setTimeout in IdleTimeoutService.startTimer is NOT .unref()'d. Project-context.md explicitly requires .unref() on long-running timers.' project-context.md line 149: 'The timer must be .unref()'d (prevents blocking clean process exit on SIGTERM where shutdown hooks may not run), and cleared in finally, stop(), and onModuleDestroy().' The circuit breaker timer in agent.service.ts follows the correct pattern (timer.unref?.()). Playbook implement-story prompt updatedAt: 2026-07-06T02:22:58.166Z (updatedByRun: 3.8-a1-20260706T003852) — the 3.8 amendment was active for this run. Ledger entry 37 (Story 3.8) records 2nd occurrence and the applied update_step. Ledger entry 5 (Story 2.2) records 1st occurrence (6 NFR fixes needed).

observations.summary: Prior hypothesized observation (ledger entry 38, Story 3.8) — the pattern did NOT recur in 3.9: validate-story-2 found 0 critical issues and 0 enhancement gaps, but the scope condition (prepare-tests creating stub files) was not met since Story 3.9 modified existing files only; the hypothesis remains open for stories where prepare-tests creates new stub files for TDD red-phase compilation.

I found 0 critical issues, 0 enhancement gaps, and 1 minor optimization (applied).' Story file line 228: 'No new files. No new Prisma models or migrations. No new modules.' Ledger entry 38 (Story 3.8) hypothesized: 'this likely recurs on any story where prepare-tests creates stub files for TDD red-phase compilation' with nextStep to check prior stories' validate-story-2 excerpts.

observations.summary: Prior hypothesized observation (ledger entry 39, Story 3.8) — the pattern did NOT recur in 3.9: the review-nfrs step found 4 NFR findings, none about incomplete regression guards, but the scope condition (creating regression guards for multiple call sites of the same method) was not met; the hypothesis remains open for stories that create such guards.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-nfrs (2026-07-06T05:28:32.797Z, status: success): 4 findings documented — HIGH (.unref() on idle timeout timer), MEDIUM (missing MANUAL_SAVE_SUCCEEDED ordering test), LOW (no upper bound on MID_SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS), LOW (missing 'reconnecting' state preservation test). None relate to incomplete regression guards across multiple call sites. Story file NFR Audit section (lines 344-350). Ledger entry 39 (Story 3.8) hypothesized: 'this may recur on future stories that create regression guards for multiple call sites of the same method' with nextStep to check prior stories' NFR-S1 or security regression guard tests.

amendments.prompt: run Validate mode. Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason). Fix stale transitional markers directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories — comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when they're actually active must be updated to reflect current state. Do not defer out-of-scope markers to a separate validation.
amendments.rationale: The review-tests step has flagged but not fixed out-of-scope stale red-phase headers in 2 stories (3.8, 3.9); the prompt's 'fix stale transitional markers directly' is being interpreted as scoped to the current story, so explicitly including markers found during the search closes the gap.

amendments.prompt: Develop story autonomously, completing every task listed in the story spec before finishing. Before marking the story complete, re-read project-context.md and verify each non-functional pattern documented there has been applied to your changes where applicable — apply any missing patterns before finishing, do not wait for the NFR review to catch them. As each task makes a skipped test pass, remove its skip marker — the story is not complete while skipped tests remain for done tasks. For any decision that arises, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md before escalating: if a rule covers it, decide and continue. HALT only for decisions no rule covers.
amendments.rationale: The implement-story step has skipped proactive NFR hardening in 3 stories (2.2, 3.8, 3.9); the 3.8 update ('apply proactively') was too passive — requiring the agent to re-read project-context.md and verify each pattern before completing is a more directive trigger.
